fields.name: note_date
fields.description: Date the note was authored/signed, format YYYY-MM-DD

fields.name: note_type
fields.description: Type of clinical note. One of: Progress | Discharge | Consultation | Admission

fields.name: document_id
fields.description: Electronic Medical Record (EMR) note identifier or encounter number if present

fields.name: provider_name
fields.description: Full name of the clinician authoring the note (e.g., "Dr. Sarah Chen, MD")

fields.name: provider_npi
fields.description: National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the authoring provider — 10-digit numeric; null if not embedded in note

fields.name: provider_specialty
fields.description: Clinical specialty or department of the provider (e.g., "Internal Medicine", "Emergency Medicine", "Orthopedics")

fields.name: patient_name
fields.description: Full name of the patient

fields.name: patient_dob
fields.description: Patient date of birth, format YYYY-MM-DD

fields.name: patient_mrn
fields.description: Medical Record Number (MRN) assigned by the facility

fields.name: facility_name
fields.description: Name of the hospital, clinic, or practice where the note was created

fields.name: department
fields.description: Clinical department or unit (e.g., "Cardiology Outpatient Clinic", "Emergency Department", "2-North Med/Surg")

fields.name: chief_complaint
fields.description: Patient's primary stated reason for visit in their own words or as paraphrased by provider. Free text, maximum 200 characters. Extract the most specific complaint from the "Chief Complaint" or "CC:" label

fields.name: subjective_summary
fields.description: Summary of the full Subjective section including history of present illness (HPI), review of systems, and relevant history. Free text, maximum 300 characters. Truncate with ellipsis if longer

fields.name: objective_vitals
fields.description: Structured object containing vital signs. Extract each of the following sub-fields as a string value (include units). Set to null if not documented:

fields.name: bp
fields.description: Blood pressure (e.g., "138/88 mmHg")

fields.name: hr
fields.description: Heart rate (e.g., "76 bpm")

fields.name: temp
fields.description: Temperature (e.g., "98.6°F" or "37.0°C")

fields.name: rr
fields.description: Respiratory rate (e.g., "16 breaths/min")

fields.name: spo2
fields.description: Oxygen saturation (e.g., "97% on room air")

fields.name: weight
fields.description: Body weight (e.g., "185 lbs" or "83.9 kg")

fields.name: assessment_diagnoses
fields.description: Array of diagnosis objects. For each diagnosis found in the Assessment section, extract:

fields.name: icd10_code
fields.description: ICD-10-CM code (e.g., "I10", "E11.9"). Null if only described without a code

fields.name: description
fields.description: Diagnosis description or name as written in the note

fields.name: status
fields.description: One of: Active | Chronic | Resolved | New | Suspected (infer from context if not explicit)

fields.name: plan_summary
fields.description: Summary of the Plan section including medications prescribed/adjusted, orders placed, referrals, and instructions. Free text, maximum 300 characters. Truncate with ellipsis if longer

fields.name: medications_mentioned
fields.description: Array of medication names referenced anywhere in the note (including current medications list, new prescriptions, discontinued medications). Include drug name only — no dosing details

fields.name: follow_up_instructions
fields.description: Specific follow-up instruction text (e.g., "Return to clinic if fever > 101.5°F or worsening symptoms")

fields.name: follow_up_timeframe
fields.description: Time interval for scheduled follow-up (e.g., "2 weeks", "3 months", "as needed")

sections.title: Dates And Terms
sections.description: Dates And Terms fields extracted from the document.

sections.title: Document Details
sections.description: Document Details fields extracted from the document.

sections.title: Parties
sections.description: Parties fields extracted from the document.

sections.title: Risk And Compliance
sections.description: Risk And Compliance fields extracted from the document.

completeness_checklist.description: All required fields are present or explicitly null with low confidence when absent.
Each declared section was reviewed for extractable evidence.
Object array fields use their item_schema for consistent nested extraction.
